config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena di situlah pintu penyalahgunaan kuasa sering terbuka. Dalam pengalaman saya, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan terhadap orang kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi, lalu kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse of power. Public needs matter, but rights like free speech, due process, and bodily autonomy are not luxuries; they are the guardrails that keep society from trampling people when the majority gets impatient or fearful. I do think there are limited cases where individual rights can be constrained for genuine public safety, like quarantine or criminal law, but those exceptions need to be narrow and justified. In my view, a healthy society is one that protects individuals first and then asks them to contribute to the common good, not one that treats the person as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” memang sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, padahal justru di situ hak warga harus paling dijaga supaya kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Kepentingan bersama itu penting, tetapi harus dibangun lewat batas yang jelas: hak dasar individu tetap dilindungi, lalu pembatasan hanya boleh terjadi kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, masyarakat malah berisiko jadi alat pembenaran untuk ketidakadilan yang dibungkus rapi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not take automatic priority over the individual, because that is exactly how governments and majorities justify overreach. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any argument that starts with “the common good” and ends with people losing free speech, due process, or bodily autonomy, since our history shows how often power gets abused when rights are treated as secondary. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every case, because public health and criminal law sometimes require limits, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. In my view, a society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and then asks people to share responsibility for the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga yang lemah. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi kalau ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara otomatis, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa, bukan kebaikan bersama. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah hak dasar individu dilindungi dulu, lalu pembatasan hanya boleh dilakukan kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan orang per orang, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan bersama tanpa menghapus martabat individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take automatic priority over the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to justify overreach and discrimination. The other side is right that public safety and shared needs matter, but those goals do not excuse treating rights like free speech, due process, and bodily autonomy as optional. I would only accept limits on individual rights when they are narrow, evidence-based, and accountable, not when they are waved through under vague claims of the common good. To me, a society is legitimate only when it protects the person first and then asks for sacrifice only when it is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
